seed_problem: congestion-pricing on a road network with equilibrium routing
mutation: composite: replace exact-answer routing with an open-ended toll-design objective under a budget, scored by the total travel time of a deterministic best-response equilibrium; plant Braess paradox gadgets so that surgically deleting shortcut edges beats any smooth congestion-proportional pricing, plus decoy gadgets where deletion hurts
theme: city road tolls before festival weekend
title: Toll Surgeon: Taming Braess Traffic Before the Festival
strategies: trivial: post no tolls (the do-nothing equilibrium, the calibration baseline)

greedy: smooth congestion-proportional pricing -- spread the whole budget over roads in proportion to their congestion sensitivity a_e; never taxes the zero-latency Braess shortcuts and mis-spends on decoy districts

recipe (stronger than greedy, weaker than strong): marginal / latency*flow proportional pricing of congested roads -- indirectly relieves each single Braess paradox but cannot concentrate the scarce budget

strong: counterfactual network surgery -- decompose into districts, binary-search the minimum toll that deletes each road, keep only deletions that lower total travel time, knapsack the scarce budget by benefit/cost, then marginal-price the surviving congested roads with the leftover
beyond strong: full social-optimum pricing of the post-surgery network and partial (non-deleting) tolls, which can cut travel time further below the split equilibrium
